us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Trump alleges China meddled in 2020 election ahead of midterms
bullets: President Donald Trump used a primetime address to accuse China of interfering in the 2020 election.
The BBC reported the US intelligence community had previously concluded China did not interfere in that election.
China’s foreign ministry denied the allegations and called them fabricated.

articles.title: Canada wildfire smoke triggers air-quality alerts across US cities
bullets: More than 800 wildfires were burning across Canada, sending smoke into the United States.
Air-quality alerts affected cities including New York, Detroit, Chicago, Pittsburgh and Toledo.
Officials cancelled outdoor events and urged vulnerable residents to stay indoors.

articles.title: Taco Bell removes lettuce in US outbreak linked to parasite
bullets: Taco Bell removed lettuce from some menus after a cyclosporiasis outbreak investigation.
US officials linked cases to shredded iceberg lettuce served at locations in five states.
The CDC reported 1,645 infections and 94 hospitalisations, with no deaths reported.

articles.title: Texas to investigate fatal ICE shooting in Houston
bullets: Texas Governor Greg Abbott said the Texas Rangers will investigate the fatal ICE shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo.
ICE agents stopped Salgado Araujo on July 7 while he was driving to work; he later died at a hospital.
DHS later said Salgado Araujo was not the target of the immigration operation.

articles.title: Rubio urges global crackdown on far-left extremism
bullets: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio hosted representatives from more than 60 countries in Washington.
The State Department announced visa restrictions tied to groups it labels far-left terrorist or aligned organisations.
Democratic lawmakers questioned whether the effort could target lawful protest or political opponents.

articles.title: US launches new Iran strikes as Tehran says civilian infrastructure was hit
bullets: Iran accused the US of striking bridges, a train station and an airport in its latest wave of attacks.
BBC Verify confirmed damage to a bridge in Hormozgan province.
Iran’s Revolutionary Guards claimed retaliatory strikes on targets linked to US forces in Oman, Kuwait, Bahrain and Syria.

articles.title: More than 500 Rohingya asylum seekers vanish at sea
bullets: Two boats carrying an estimated 530 Rohingya left Myanmar’s Rakhine state on June 29.
Rights workers told the BBC the vessels likely capsized during rough monsoon seas.
Families had not heard from passengers nearly three weeks after departure.

articles.title: Japan eases imperial succession rules but keeps female-emperor ban
bullets: Japan’s parliament approved changes allowing adoption of distant male relatives into the imperial family.
Women in the imperial family will be able to keep royal status after marrying outside the family.
The bill does not allow women, including Princess Aiko, to inherit the throne.

articles.title: Uganda suspends school trips after deadly bus crash
bullets: Uganda suspended all school trips after a bus crash killed at least 20 pupils and one adult.
The bus was carrying children from King David Junior School on a field trip in Kapchorwa district.
Officials said preliminary findings pointed to a mechanical fault before the driver lost control.

articles.title: Australia criticises Laos over expected methanol-poisoning charges
bullets: Australia said it was deeply frustrated by expected Laos charges over the deaths of six backpackers in 2024.
Australians Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles were among those who died after drinking methanol-tainted alcohol.
Australia summoned Laos’ ambassador in Canberra over the reported handling of the case.

articles.title: Four students among five dead after train hits school van in Murshidabad
bullets: A train rammed a school van at a crossing in West Bengal’s Murshidabad district.
The Indian Express reported four students were among five people killed.
Railway officials said strict action was taken and the gateman was arrested.

articles.title: Sonam Wangchuk refuses to end 20-day hunger strike
bullets: Activist Sonam Wangchuk said he would continue his indefinite fast at Jantar Mantar in Delhi.
The BBC reported he had consumed only salt water for 20 days and lost more than 9 kg.
The Delhi High Court ordered the government to monitor his health and provide treatment if needed.

articles.title: Modi flags off India’s first hydrogen-powered train
bullets: Prime Minister Narendra Modi flagged off India’s first hydrogen-powered train between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana.
The train includes leak detectors, auto shutdown and real-time safety monitoring systems.
The project is part of Indian Railways’ push toward lower-emission transport technology.

articles.title: NEET UG 2026 results released after re-exam
bullets: The National Testing Agency released NEET UG 2026 results and scorecards.
The Indian Express reported two students jointly shared All India Rank 1.
The results followed a re-exam after the May test was cancelled.

articles.title: Gates MRI and Serum Institute partner on TB vaccine candidate manufacturing
bullets: Gates MRI and Serum Institute of India announced a tie-up for a tuberculosis vaccine candidate.
The Pune-based manufacturer will support production tied to the vaccine programme.
The partnership comes as TB prevention remains a major public-health priority in India and globally.

articles.title: Alphabet in focus after EU orders Google to open services to AI and search rivals
bullets: Reuters reported EU regulators set out new requirements for Google under Big Tech competition rules.
The changes would require Google to help AI and search rivals access some services.
Investing.com’s Reuters page listed GOOGL up 3.17% alongside the report.

articles.title: Trump Media plans paid high-speed Truth Social data feed
bullets: Trump Media & Technology Group said it will sell fast access to high-ranking Truth Social posts.
The BBC reported the Truth API will deliver posts to institutional clients in milliseconds from August 1.
The company said the feed could become a new revenue stream for the loss-making business.

articles.title: Paramount Skydance shares remain watched after antitrust-report pressure
bullets: Axios reported Paramount Skydance fell 4.3% after a Reuters report on potential state antitrust action.
The reported lawsuit would challenge the company’s Warner Bros. acquisition.
The move kept media consolidation risk in focus for investors.

articles.title: Capri named among midday market movers as retail stocks draw attention
bullets: CNBC’s market coverage listed Capri among stocks making big midday moves.
The move came during a session marked by rotation away from some megacap technology names.
Retail names remained sensitive to tariff, consumer-spending and earnings expectations.

articles.title: Cava listed among stocks making big midday moves
bullets: CNBC listed Cava among notable midday movers in US trading.
Restaurant and consumer names were in focus as investors weighed spending trends.
The move came alongside broader market rotation into smaller and non-megacap names.

articles.title: China condemns UK nationalisation of British Steel
bullets: China said it strongly opposed the UK government’s decision to nationalise British Steel.
The UK said public ownership would protect jobs and a vital domestic steelmaking capability.
The Scunthorpe steelworks directly employs about 2,700 people and supports more supply-chain jobs.

articles.title: Trump Media to monetise market-moving social posts
bullets: Trump Media will sell a commercial data feed called Truth API to institutional clients.
The BBC reported the service is aimed at traders seeking faster access to posts that can move markets.
The company did not disclose the price for the service.

articles.title: Oil rises as US-Iran hostilities threaten key shipping routes
bullets: Reuters reported oil prices inched up as US-Iran hostilities intensified across the Gulf.
The report cited risks to flows through the Strait of Hormuz and potential Houthi action near the Red Sea.
Energy markets remained sensitive to disruptions affecting major oil-export routes.

articles.title: UK says British Steel takeover protects national supply
bullets: The UK government said it brought British Steel into public ownership to secure steelmaking capability.
The Steel Industry Nationalisation Act received Royal Assent on July 15, 2026.
Officials said continued production was in the national interest.

articles.title: Indian rupee posts sharp weekly drop as oil jump stings
bullets: Reuters reported the Indian rupee logged its sharpest weekly decline in nine weeks.
Higher oil prices weighed on the currency because India is a major crude importer.
The move reflected market concern over energy costs tied to Gulf tensions.

articles.title: EU requires Google to open services to AI and search rivals
bullets: EU regulators said Google must help AI and search competitors access its services.
The Reuters report named OpenAI and other AI rivals as potential beneficiaries of the changes.
The move is part of EU enforcement aimed at curbing Big Tech market power.

articles.title: Scientists warn of growing orbital-debris cloud
bullets: The Indian Express reported a newly identified concentration of orbital debris raised satellite-safety concerns.
The story highlighted worries about the long-term sustainability of space operations.
Space-junk risk remains a growing issue for communications, navigation and Earth-observation satellites.

articles.title: Publishers sue Google over copyrighted books used for AI training
bullets: The Indian Express archive listed a lawsuit by publishers against Google over AI training data.
The case concerns alleged use of copyrighted books to train artificial-intelligence systems.
The suit adds to legal scrutiny over how AI companies source training material.

articles.title: Trump Media launches instant social-post API for institutions
bullets: Truth API will deliver Truth Social posts to paying clients in milliseconds.
The BBC reported the service will operate 24 hours a day, seven days a week.
The launch reflects growing demand for machine-readable data tied to market-moving political posts.

articles.title: Meta pushes AI monetisation with Muse Spark developer access
bullets: Axios reported Meta planned to charge developers for access to its Muse Spark AI model update.
The update is part of Meta’s effort to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic in AI tools.
Meta’s AI revenue plans follow reports it is exploring cloud-computing services using spare data-centre capacity.
